title: euclea racemosa subsp. daphnoides
intro: Euclea racemosa subsp. daphnoides, also known as the Horned Euclea or Daphnoid Euclea, is a species of flowering plant in the family Ebenaceae. This plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Euclea racemosa subsp. daphnoides include the potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support cardiovascular health. In traditional herbal medicine, it is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and may also be used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
